title: Green Curry Salmon with Cashews
description: Salmon fillets poached in a coconut green curry with bok choy, finished with lime, scallions, cilantro, and roasted cashews. 35 minutes, one pan.

ingredients.name: bok choy (about 1.5 lbs), stems sliced, leaves cut into 2-inch pieces

instructions.text: If serving with rice, start it now.
Season salmon on all sides with 1 tsp salt. Set aside.
Combine coconut milk, curry paste, ginger, garlic, and remaining 1 tsp salt in a large high-sided skillet over medium heat. Cook, stirring occasionally, until simmering, 5–6 minutes.

Cut bok choy stems into 1/2-inch slices and leaves into 2-inch pieces. Rinse well and drain. Add to the coconut milk mixture and stir to coat. Nestle salmon fillets into the bok choy in an even layer.
Cover pan and cook over medium-low heat until salmon is just cooked through and flesh is opaque, 6–8 minutes. Remove from heat and pour lime juice over salmon.
Scatter scallions, cilantro, cashews, and chile (if using) over the top. Serve with rice alongside if using.

notes:
Full-fat coconut milk is important here -- light coconut milk won't give you the same richness in the curry base.

Green curry paste brands vary significantly in heat. Taste your paste before adding -- if it's very spicy, start with 3 tbsp and adjust.

Works well over rice or on its own as a lower-carb option with just the bok choy.
